inverts_input_built_from_1_pmos_1_nmos: Inverts input. Built from 1 PMOS + 1 NMOS (CMOS inverter).
output_high_only_when_both_inputs_are_: Output HIGH only when BOTH inputs are HIGH. NAND + NOT (6 transistors).
output_high_when_any_input_is_high_nor: Output HIGH when ANY input is HIGH. NOR + NOT (6 transistors).
universal_gate_2_series_nmos_2_paralle: Universal gate! 2 series NMOS + 2 parallel PMOS. All logic from NANDs.
universal_gate_2_parallel_nmos_2_serie: Universal gate! 2 parallel NMOS + 2 series PMOS.
output_high_when_inputs_differ_key_for: Output HIGH when inputs differ. Key for adders. Uses ~8 transistors.
output_high_when_inputs_match_used_in_: Output HIGH when inputs match. Used in comparators.
build_a_circuit_from_components_add_pa: Build a circuit from components. Add parts, then simulate!
click_components_above_to_add_them: Click components above to add them
a_silicon_ingot_is_pulled_from_molten_: A silicon ingot is pulled from molten silicon using the Czochralski method at ~1414°C.
the_ingot_is_sliced_into_thin_wafers_7: The ingot is sliced into thin wafers (~775μm thick) using diamond wire saws.
a_thin_layer_of_sio_is_grown_on_the_wa: A thin layer of SiO₂ is grown on the wafer surface at ~1000°C in an oxygen furnace.
uv_light_transfers_circuit_patterns_fr: UV light transfers circuit patterns from a mask onto photoresist-coated wafers.
unwanted_material_is_removed_by_chemic: Unwanted material is removed by chemical (wet) or plasma (dry) etching.
dopant_atoms_are_shot_into_the_silicon: Dopant atoms are shot into the silicon at high energy to change its electrical properties.
metal_and_insulator_layers_are_deposit: Metal and insulator layers are deposited by CVD, PVD, or ALD techniques.
metal_interconnects_are_patterned_and_: Metal interconnects are patterned and polished flat. Multiple layers build up the circuit wiring.
moore_s_law_graph_showing_transistor_c: Moore's Law graph showing transistor counts from 1965 to 2030
6_transistors_per_bit_2_cross_coupled_: 6 transistors per bit: 2 cross-coupled inverters + 2 access transistors. Used in CPU caches.
1_transistor_1_capacitor_per_bit_must_: 1 transistor + 1 capacitor per bit. Must refresh every ~64ms. Used in main memory (RAM sticks).
floating_gate_transistor_traps_charge_: Floating-gate transistor traps charge. NOR: random access, used in firmware/BIOS.
series_connected_floating_gates_sequen: Series-connected floating gates. Sequential access but extremely dense. SSDs, USB drives, SD cards.
ferroelectric_capacitor_stores_charge_: Ferroelectric capacitor stores charge with remnant polarization. Non-volatile + fast write. Used in smart cards, automotive.
voltage_amplifier_high_input_impedance: Voltage amplifier. High input impedance (∞ for ideal). Gain = -gₘRₓ. Inverts signal. Most common MOSFET amp.
voltage_buffer_gain_1_no_inversion_low: Voltage buffer. Gain ≈ 1 (no inversion). Low output impedance → good for driving loads. Also called source follower.
current_buffer_low_input_impedance_non: Current buffer. Low input impedance. Non-inverting. Wide bandwidth → good for RF applications.
bjt_voltage_amplifier_gain_g_r_inverts: BJT voltage amplifier. Gain = -gₘRᶜ. Inverts signal. Higher gain than MOSFET but lower input impedance.
amplifies_difference_of_two_inputs_rej: Amplifies difference of two inputs. Rejects common-mode noise. Foundation of op-amps. CMRR typically >60dB.
band_gaps_between_0_5_and_3_5_ev_are_s: Band gaps between 0.5 and 3.5 eV are semiconductors.
extra_electrons_n_for_negative_charge_: Extra electrons = N for Negative charge carriers.
think_of_it_as_multiplication_1_1_1_ev: Think of it as multiplication: 1×1=1, everything else=0.
carriers_recombine_at_the_junction_lea: Carriers recombine at the junction leaving fixed ions.
positive_gate_attracts_electrons_to_fo: Positive gate attracts electrons to form a channel.
forward_pushing_carriers_toward_the_ju: Forward = pushing carriers TOWARD the junction.
more_thermal_energy_more_electron_hole: More thermal energy = more electron-hole pairs.
nand_can_implement_not_and_or_everythi: NAND can implement NOT, AND, OR — everything!
pmos_nmos_work_as_a_complementary_pair: PMOS + NMOS work as a complementary pair.
speed_mobility_gaas_8500_vs_si_1400_cm: Speed ∝ mobility. GaAs: 8500 vs Si: 1400 cm²/Vs.
zener_diodes_are_designed_to_conduct_i: Zener diodes are designed to conduct in reverse!
the_o_in_mos_stands_for_oxide_insulato: The "O" in MOS stands for Oxide (insulator).
boron_has_3_valence_electrons_1_fewer_: Boron has 3 valence electrons (1 fewer than Si).
in_cmos_one_transistor_is_always_off_b: In CMOS, one transistor is always OFF blocking current.
n_1_for_ideal_junction_n_2_for_recombi: n=1 for ideal junction, n=2 for recombination in depletion region.
e_3_4_ev_allows_higher_voltage_operati: Eₗ = 3.4 eV allows higher voltage operation.
sum_a_xor_b_carry_a_and_b: Sum = A XOR B, Carry = A AND B.
higher_v_ce_widens_the_depletion_regio: Higher V_CE widens the depletion region into the base.
e_1_l_smaller_box_higher_energy_steps: E ∝ 1/L² — smaller box = higher energy steps.
telecom_lasers_use_ingaasp_quantum_wel: Telecom lasers use InGaAsP quantum wells for precise wavelength control.
dram_stores_bits_as_charge_on_a_tiny_c: DRAM stores bits as charge on a tiny capacitor — it leaks!
the_floating_gate_is_electrically_isol: The floating gate is electrically isolated by oxide layers.
l1_cache_needs_the_fastest_possible_ac: L1 cache needs the fastest possible access time.
it_s_a_buffer_unity_gain_impedance_tra: It's a buffer: unity gain, impedance transformation.
higher_gain_lower_bandwidth_a_bw_gbw_c: Higher gain = lower bandwidth. Aᵥ × BW = GBW = constant.
a_swarm_of_logic_bugs_what_gate_output: A swarm of logic bugs! What gate outputs 1 when A=0?
a_voltage_spike_is_overloading_which_d: A voltage spike is overloading! Which device regulates voltage?
temperature_rising_in_a_semiconductor_: Temperature rising! In a semiconductor, increasing temp does what to conductivity?
an_enemy_flipped_all_bits_which_gate_c: An enemy flipped all bits! Which gate can invert every signal?
carriers_are_leaking_what_narrows_the_: Carriers are leaking! What narrows the depletion region?
electrostatic_discharge_what_protects_: Electrostatic discharge! What protects ICs from ESD?
clock_is_unstable_cmos_power_dissipati: Clock is unstable! CMOS power dissipation is proportional to:
direct_short_what_is_the_threshold_vol: Direct short! What is the threshold voltage of a typical Si MOSFET?
electrons_are_tunneling_through_the_ox: Electrons are tunneling through the oxide! In a quantum well, narrower well =
memory_bits_are_flipping_which_memory_: Memory bits are flipping! Which memory type needs refreshing?
signal_to_noise_ratio_dropping_what_do: Signal-to-noise ratio dropping! What does a differential amplifier reject?
final_boss_what_limits_transistor_scal: Final boss! What limits transistor scaling below ~1nm gate oxide?
protect_your_chip_from_waves_of_hardwa: Protect your chip from waves of hardware enemies! Use semiconductor knowledge to fight back.
doping_silicon_with_phosphorus_n_type_: Doping silicon with phosphorus (n-type) or boron (p-type) is the foundation of every chip ever made. The PN junction is the building block of every diode + LED + solar cell + transistor.
bias_a_transistor_calculate_band_gap_e: Bias a transistor, calculate band-gap energy, predict current vs voltage. AP Physics 2 + intro EE problems with step-by-step feedback.
time_pressure_rounds_build_a_circuit_f: Time-pressure rounds: build a circuit faster than the timer. Tests whether semiconductor reasoning is automatic, not just recognized.
bardeen_brattain_shockley_invented_the: Bardeen + Brattain + Shockley invented the transistor at Bell Labs (1947); Nobel 1956. Moore's Law: transistor count doubles every ~2 years; held for 50+ years before slowing.
minimal_dopant_pure_semiconductor_high: Minimal dopant. Pure semiconductor — high resistivity.
some_carriers_introduced_typical_extri: Some carriers introduced. Typical extrinsic regime.
approaching_metallic_behavior_used_for: Approaching metallic behavior. Used for contacts.
adjust_dopant_concentration_temperatur: Adjust dopant concentration, temperature, material. Discrete 4-band regime. No score, no reveal.
hypothesis_how_does_temperature_affect: Hypothesis: How does temperature affect carrier concentration?
compare_si_and_gaas_at_same_concentrat: Compare Si and GaAs at same concentration. Why differ?
find_two_settings_producing_same_regim: Find two settings producing same regime.
i_understand_explain_in_own_words: I understand — explain in own words
explain_how_concentration_temperature_: Explain how concentration, temperature, and material jointly set the regime.
design_note_discrete_4_state_marker_no: Design note: discrete 4-state marker; no carrier-density score; no reveal — by design.
